articles.title: UK Leader Stands Up for Britain After US President's Comments
articles.summary: The UK's Foreign Secretary, Yvette Cooper, says Prime Minister Keir Starmer is doing the right thing by protecting Britain's interests. This comes after US President Donald Trump criticized the UK's response to a conflict involving Iran. Trump posted on social media that the UK might send aircraft carriers to the Middle East, but he seemed to think it was unnecessary. Cooper explained that the UK government makes its own decisions about what is best for Britain, instead of always agreeing with other countries like the US. The UK has allowed the US to use British bases for defense and sent RAF jets to help shoot down missiles. Some people, like former Prime Minister Tony Blair, think the UK should have supported the US more from the start. Cooper says it's important to learn from past mistakes, like the Iraq War, and focus on what is right for British citizens. The Iranian ambassador warned the UK to be careful about getting more involved.

articles.title: Exploring Hidden Ocean Treasures in the Caribbean
articles.summary: Scientists have gone on an exciting adventure in the Caribbean Sea near islands like the Cayman Islands. They used special cameras to explore deep underwater where no one has been before. They discovered amazing things like a huge underwater mountain, a deep blue hole, colorful coral reefs, and strange sea creatures like a glowing eel and a fish with see-through eyes. The team worked day and night for six weeks, going as deep as 6,000 meters. They found many new animals and want to protect this beautiful place from pollution and climate change. The scientists are happy because they saw things nobody knew existed, and they hope to learn more to keep the ocean safe.

articles.title: Why Being Too Sure Can Be Scary
articles.summary: A TV and radio host shares his thoughts after talking to many people for over 50 years. He says that today, people are too certain about their opinions and don't listen to others. In the past, radio shows were more formal, but now, phone-in shows let ordinary people share their views. The host notices that arguments have become more angry and divided, especially after events like Brexit. People often dislike those who disagree with them, making it hard to have friendly talks. He warns that being too sure can stop us from understanding different ideas and getting along.

articles.title: King Charles Talks About Friendship and Problems in the World
articles.summary: King Charles III is giving a special message for Commonwealth Day, which is a big celebration for 56 countries that work together. He talks about how important it is to have friends and alliances when there are conflicts and wars happening in places like the Middle East and Ukraine. The King also mentions climate change, which is a big problem for our planet. He says the Commonwealth helps bring people together and cares for the Earth. There will be a service at Westminster Abbey with music, dance, and readings to celebrate. Some famous people like Geri Halliwell-Horner and Oti Mabuse will perform. The King might visit the US soon, but it's not sure yet. The service won't be on TV this year because of money issues.

articles.title: Why Gas Prices Are Going Up Because of a War
articles.summary: A war in the Middle East is making oil and gas prices rise quickly. The U.S. and Israel attacked Iran, and now many countries in the Middle East are fighting. This war has damaged oil facilities and stopped ships from carrying oil through the Persian Gulf. When less oil can be shipped, it becomes harder to get, so prices go up. For example, oil prices jumped to over $90 a barrel, and gasoline prices in the U.S. increased by about 43 cents in a week. People are paying more at gas stations, and some worry the war might last a long time. This affects everyone because higher gas prices mean it costs more to drive cars, fly planes, and heat homes.

articles.title: Winter Paralympics: 6 Fun Sports for Athletes
articles.summary: The Winter Paralympics have started in Italy with a big opening ceremony. Over 600 athletes are competing for 79 medals in six exciting sports. These sports are Para alpine skiing, Para biathlon, Para cross-country skiing, Para ice hockey, Para snowboard, and wheelchair curling. In skiing sports, athletes can compete in three groups: standing, sitting on a special ski, or with vision impairment using a guide. They race in different events like slalom and downhill. The games began in 1976 and are held in cool places like Cortina. It's all about showing amazing skills and having fun in the snow!
